source_jobs.title: Senior iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.



Role Description

We're looking for a talented Senior iOS Engineer for our mobile team to join Bankjoy! You will be responsible for developing native iOS code solutions to build digital banking products and features with best UI and UX practices.

In this role, you will:

 - Partner cross-functionally with product, design, and engineering to deliver high-quality, user-centric iOS experiences

 - Design, develop, and maintain iOS applications using Swift with a strong focus on performance, scalability, and usability

 - Architect and implement robust, maintainable solutions aligned with best practices and long-term product goals

 - Participate in feature planning, technical design, and effort estimation

 - Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve complex product and performance issues

 - Maintain and improve code quality through testing, code reviews, and continuous refactoring

 - Write and maintain unit tests to ensure reliability and stability

 - Keep the codebase current with evolving iOS platforms, frameworks, and industry standards

 - Maintain and enhance CI/CD pipelines and development workflows

 - Document code, systems, and processes to support team knowledge sharing

 - Mentor junior engineers and contribute to a strong engineering culture

 - Stay current with emerging iOS technologies, tools, and trends

What you bring:

 - 7+ years of professional software development experience, including 5+ years focused on iOS development

 - Strong proficiency in Swift and modern iOS frameworks (UIKit, SwiftUI, Combine, etc.)

 - Proven experience designing, building, and shipping high-quality, scalable iOS applications

 - Strong understanding of software architecture patterns (MVVM, MVC, VIPER, etc.) and best practices

 - Solid experience integrating RESTful APIs, backend services, and third-party libraries

 - Strong understanding of UI/UX principles and building intuitive user experiences

 - Demonstrated ability to lead technical initiatives and deliver projects end-to-end

 - Experience mentoring engineers, conducting code reviews, and improving team code quality

 - Strong cross-functional collaboration skills with product, design, and backend teams

 - Ability to make sound technical decisions and balance trade-offs in fast-paced environments

 - Solid experience with testing practices (unit testing, UI testing, TDD) and maintaining high code quality standards

 - Proficiency with Git and modern development workflows

 - Track record of success in high-growth or startup environments, demonstrating ownership and adaptability

 - Exceptional problem-solving skills, strong communication, and a collaborative mindset

 - Experience with modern AI tools

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or equivalent experience




🎁 WHAT YOU GET

 - A fast-paced and collaborative environment 

 - Competitive compensation

 - Stock options at a well-funded startup

 - We cover 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums for individuals on our base plan, with significant contributions toward all other plan options (USA).

 - We cover 100% of extended medical, dental, and vision premiums for you and your family under our single, comprehensive plan (Canada).

 - Retirement plan with 4% company matching

 - 8 weeks paid parental leave for birthing parents, 4 weeks for non-birthing

 - 3-5 weeks PTO depending on tenure

 - 5 health days

 - 15 holidays

 - End of year shutdown

 - Performance-based bonuses

source_jobs.title: Lead Android Engineer
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.




THE ROLE

As Android Tech Lead at Bankjoy, you will own the technical direction, architecture, and delivery of our Android banking platform while leading a team of three Android engineers embedded across our digital banking value streams.

This is a hands-on leadership role. You will write production code daily, drive architectural decisions, and ensure our Android product meets the reliability, security, and performance standards that financial institutions and their members depend on. You will work cross-functionally with Product Management, iOS, QA, and backend engineering to deliver a seamless, high-quality mobile banking experience.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

Technical Leadership & Architecture

 - Own the Android technical roadmap — architectural decisions, technology choices, and long-term platform direction

 - Define and enforce Android engineering standards across the team including code quality, testing practices, and release readiness

 - Lead solution design for new features and major platform initiatives, ensuring scalability, security, and maintainability

 - Drive adoption of modern Android development practices — Jetpack Compose, MVVM/Clean Architecture, coroutines, and dependency injection

 - Partner with iOS Tech Lead and backend engineering to ensure consistent cross-platform product behavior and API contract alignment

Execution & Delivery

 - Own Android delivery across digital banking value streams — planning, estimation, milestone tracking, and risk management

 - Break down complex initiatives into executable engineering tasks with clear dependencies and timelines

 - Proactively identify and resolve technical blockers before they impact release schedules

 - Collaborate with QA to define Android-specific testing strategy — unit, integration, and UI automation using Espresso or equivalent

 - Participate in the on-call rotation for production support, responding to urgent issues impacting financial institution clients and their members

Hands-On Development

 - Contribute directly to feature development, code reviews, debugging, and performance optimization within the digital banking value stream

 - Maintain a meaningful individual coding contribution while balancing technical leadership responsibilities

 - Champion clean, testable, well-documented Kotlin code across the Android codebase

Team Leadership & Development

 - Directly manage three Android engineers — conducting regular 1:1s, providing performance feedback, and supporting their technical growth

 - Foster a collaborative, high-trust team environment that emphasizes engineering excellence and continuous learning

 - Mentor engineers on Android best practices, fintech domain knowledge, and effective cross-functional collaboration

 - Partner with engineering leadership on hiring, onboarding, and growing Android engineering capability

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Product Management and Design to shape feature scope, assess feasibility, and provide accurate effort estimates

 - Collaborate with backend engineers on API design and integration patterns for mobile banking flows

 - Align with QA on automation coverage strategy — supporting the organization's migration to modern mobile testing frameworks

 - Partner with DevOps on Android build pipelines, release automation, and CI/CD integration

Security & Compliance

 - Strictly adhere to security protocols when handling customer financial data

 - Apply knowledge o

source_jobs.title: Senior Marketing Designer (Part time, contract)
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.



The Role

We are looking for a Senior Marketing Designer to own the visual execution of Bankjoy's brand across marketing channels. This is a part-time contractor engagement of approximately 20 hours per week, with the flexibility to scale during high-demand periods such as product launches, trade shows, and our annual client conference.

You will work closely with our Senior Product Marketing Manager and broader marketing team to produce high-quality creative assets that bring our brand and product story to life. The ideal candidate is a strong visual communicator with deep fintech or B2B SaaS design experience, a sharp eye for detail, and the ability to work independently with minimal direction.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


Brand & Marketing Design

 - Design and produce marketing collateral across digital and print channels including presentation decks, one-pagers, brochures, email templates, social media assets, digital ads, and landing page visuals.

 - Maintain and evolve Bankjoy's brand identity, ensuring consistency across all touchpoints.

 - Develop and maintain a library of reusable design templates that empower the broader team to produce on-brand content independently.
   

Product Marketing Support

 - Collaborate with the Product Marketing team to design compelling visual narratives for product launches, feature announcements, and sales enablement materials.

 - Create visually engaging pitch decks, client presentations, and RFP response materials.

 - Design assets for webinars, virtual events, and thought leadership content.
   

Event & Conference Design

 - Produce event-specific creative including booth graphics, signage, banners, swag concepts, and conference materials for trade shows and Bankjoy's annual client conference (Elevate).

 - Ensure all event materials are on-brand and delivered on time.
   

Digital & Social

 - Design social media graphics, LinkedIn content, and digital advertising assets.

 - Support website visual updates and landing page design in collaboration with the marketing team.
   


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS


Must Have

 - 6+ years of marketing design experience, ideally in B2B SaaS, fintech, or financial services.

 - Strong portfolio demonstrating range across brand, digital, and print design.

 - Expert-level proficiency in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, InDesign, Photoshop).

 - Experience working directly with marketing and product marketing teams in a fast-paced startup or growth-stage company.

 - Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously, meet deadlines, and work independently without heavy oversight.

 - Strong communication skills — comfortable giving and receiving feedback and proactively flagging blockers.


Nice to Have

 - Experience designing for credit union, community banking, or broader financial services brands.

 - Motion graphics or video editing capabilities.

 - Experience with Webflow, HubSpot, or similar marketing platforms.

 - Familiarity with designing for trade show and conference environments.
   


WHAT TO EXPECT

 - Approximately 20 hours per week on average, with flexibility to scale during product launches, major campaigns, and event seasons.

 - Fully remote engagement with occasional collaboration calls during core hours (11am–4pm EST).

 - Close collaboration with the Senior Product Marketing Manager on priorities and creative direction.

Opportunity to have meaningful creative ownership in a growing fintech brand.

Pleas

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.



We are looking to add a Senior Account Executive to our growing team who wants to be part of the excitement as we continue to scale.

At Bankjoy, we partner with community banks and credit unions to deliver modern digital banking solutions that help them attract new accounts, deepen relationships, and grow deposits. Our platform enables financial institutions to compete with large banks by delivering secure, intuitive, and fully integrated digital experiences.

In this role, you will be responsible for driving new business, building strong relationships with financial institutions, and positioning Bankjoy as a strategic technology partner.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own the full sales cycle from prospecting through close, including outbound outreach, discovery, tailored demonstrations, scoping, pricing, contract negotiation, and successful handoff to implementation

 - Build and execute a strategic territory plan to consistently achieve and exceed sales quota targets within our ideal customer profile (community banks and credit unions)

 - Generate net-new pipeline through targeted outbound prospecting and relationship-building efforts

 - Effectively position Bankjoy’s digital banking platform and solutions against competitors in active opportunities

 - Navigate complex buying processes and engage key stakeholders, including executive leadership and technical decision-makers

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with Marketing, Product, Implementation, and Customer Success to develop winning strategies for prospective clients

 - Understand each prospect’s business goals and position Bankjoy’s solutions as a strategic driver of growth, engagement, and operational efficiency

 - Travel to client and prospect locations to deliver presentations, build relationships, and support deal progression

 - Represent Bankjoy at national and regional conferences, industry events, and trade shows to build pipeline and strengthen brand awareness


WHAT YOU BRING:

 - 5+ years of experience as an Enterprise Account Executive or similar quota-carrying role

 - Demonstrated success selling enterprise SaaS solutions, ideally within fintech, digital banking, or financial services technology

 - Proven track record of managing pipeline and closing complex, high-value enterprise contracts

 - Strong negotiation, forecasting, and deal management skills

 - Experience navigating mid-market and enterprise organizations with multiple stakeholders

 - Ability to build relationships quickly with senior leaders and executive decision-makers

 - Excellent communication and presentation skills, including experience presenting to senior management and C-level executives

 - Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple opportunities simultaneously at various stages of the sales cycle

 - Experience selling to financial institutions (banks, credit unions, fintech) strongly preferred

 - Bachelor’s degree preferred

 - Willingness and ability to travel as needed




🎁 WHAT YOU GET

 - A fast-paced and collaborative environment 

 - Competitive compensation

 - Stock options at a well-funded startup

 - We cover 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums for individuals on our base plan, with significant contributions toward all other plan options (USA).

 - We cover 100% of extended medical, dental, and vision premiums for you and your family under our single, comprehensive plan (Canada).

 - Retirement plan with 4% company matchin

source_jobs.title: Talent Acquisition Partner
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.



The Role
As a Talent Acquisition Partner, you will lead full-cycle recruitment for technical, operational growth roles. You’ll partner with hiring managers to identify needs, build pipelines, and manage the hiring process from start to finish. Given our hyper-growth environment, we’re looking for someone with experience recruiting at fast-paced, high-growth tech companies, capable of sourcing top talent in a competitive market.

What you’ll do:

 - Own the end-to-end recruitment lifecycle, from role scoping and proactive sourcing to pipeline management, candidate engagement, offer negotiations, and pre-boarding administration and background checks for Canadian and US roles.

 - Partner with department heads across engineering, product, marketing, and sales to define talent needs and design sourcing strategies.

 - Proactively source candidates through platforms like LinkedIn, industry networks, and other relevant channels.

 - Ensure excellent candidate experience by managing communication and feedback throughout the hiring process.

 - Build a strong and diverse talent pipeline to meet Bankjoy’s immediate and future hiring needs.

 - Work closely with leadership to drive recruitment efforts and meet aggressive hiring targets in a hyper-growth environment.

 - Maintain recruiting metrics and deliver progress reports to senior leadership.

What you bring:

 - 5+ years of experience in full-cycle recruiting, with a focus on technical and growth roles.

 - Proven track record of success recruiting in hyper-growth tech companies.

 - Experience working in fast-paced, remote-first environments.

 - Strong understanding of recruitment strategies for engineering, product, marketing, and sales roles.

 - Excellent communication, organization, and negotiation skills.

 - Proficient with candidate sourcing recruiting tools (e.g., LinkedIn Recruiter, ATS) and sourcing strategies.

 - Ability to manage multiple roles and deliver under tight deadlines ( ave 5-15 open roles).

 - Experience with Ashby strongly prefered.

This position pays between $90,000 - $115,000 (+10% bonus and meaningful equity)




🎁 WHAT YOU GET

 - A fast-paced and collaborative environment 

 - Competitive compensation

 - Stock options at a well-funded startup

 - We cover 100% of medical, dental, and vision premiums for individuals on our base plan, with significant contributions toward all other plan options (USA).

 - We cover 100% of extended medical, dental, and vision premiums for you and your family under our single, comprehensive plan (Canada).

 - Retirement plan with 4% company matching

 - 8 weeks paid parental leave for birthing parents, 4 weeks for non-birthing

 - 3-5 weeks PTO depending on tenure

 - 5 health days

 - 15 holidays

 - End of year shutdown

 - Performance-based bonuses

 - Remote first culture
   
    


⚖️ EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITIES AT BANKJOY

Bankjoy is committed to hiring talented and qualified individuals with diverse backgrounds for all of its tech, non-tech, and leadership roles. Bankjoy provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability.



#LI-Remote

source_jobs.title: Director of Implementation
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.




THE ROLE

We are looking for a Director of Implementation to lead and scale Bankjoy's implementation organization as we grow our footprint across community banks and credit unions nationwide.

This is a senior leadership role — not an individual contributor position. You will own the implementation function end-to-end: building and developing a high-performance team of Implementation Managers and Specialists, establishing scalable delivery processes, and ensuring every client launches successfully and on time. You will be the senior point of accountability for implementation quality across Bankjoy's entire client portfolio.

The ideal candidate is a hands-on leader who has already led an implementation or professional services team at a fintech or digital banking company. You don't just manage from above — you understand the work deeply, stay close to active projects, roll up your sleeves to remove blockers, and are present when your team needs you most. You know how to build process without bureaucracy, hold a team accountable with empathy, and represent Bankjoy with credibility and composure when escalations arise.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

Team Leadership & Performance

 - Build and develop a high-performance implementation team — setting a high bar for delivery quality, accountability, and professional growth across Implementation Managers and Specialists

 - Lead through presence and example — you understand every process your team runs, from project kickoff to go-live, and can step in to coach or cover when needed

 - Set clear performance expectations and hold team members accountable to delivery standards, professional conduct, and client outcomes

 - Conduct structured 1:1s, performance reviews, and coaching conversations that develop your team's capability and career growth over time

 - Identify skill gaps on the team and proactively address them through coaching, hiring, or process improvement

 - Hire and onboard implementation talent as Bankjoy's client portfolio grows

 - Build a team culture of urgency, ownership, and continuous improvement



Hands-On Delivery & Blocker Removal

 - Stay close to active client implementations — maintaining real-time awareness of project status, risk indicators, and team workload across the full portfolio

 - Proactively identify blockers — whether client-side, cross-functional, or internal — and drive resolution before they impact timelines

 - Work directly with Product, Engineering, and Operations to unblock your team on technical issues, integration dependencies, and product gaps

 - Define, document, and continuously improve Bankjoy's implementation methodology, playbooks, and project templates — grounded in firsthand knowledge of what works in the field

 - Drive early-stage scope definition and gap analysis discipline to prevent late-stage surprises and go-live delays

 - Build and maintain reporting that gives leadership visibility into portfolio health, at-risk projects, and team capacity



Escalation Management

 - Serve as Bankjoy's senior point of escalation for implementation issues — engaging directly with client executives and internal leadership when situations require it

 - Handle escalations with composure, speed, and decisiveness — de-escalating client concerns while driving internal accountability to resolve root causes

 - Establish clear escalation protocols so your team knows when and how to surface issues, and what to expect when they do



Cross-Fun

source_jobs.title: VP of Engineering
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.




THE ROLE

We are looking for a VP of Engineering to lead four of Bankjoy's eight engineering value streams and own the quality engineering framework across the entire engineering organization.

This is a hands-on leadership role at a critical inflection point in Bankjoy's growth. You will directly lead engineering teams across Banking Core Integration, Business Banking, Vendor Integration, and Client Analytics — while building the quality standards, automation framework, and release discipline that raise the bar for all eight value streams.

The ideal candidate has deep roots in quality engineering, has personally rescued or rebuilt automation programs in multi-platform environments, and has the organizational credibility to drive a shift from manual-heavy to automation-first quality culture. You have led engineering managers and senior ICs, partnered closely with technical support and release operations, and know what it takes to deliver reliable software to regulated financial institutions.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Engineering Leadership

 - Lead four value streams — Banking Core Integration, Business Banking, Vendor Integration, and Account Acquisition — owning delivery, velocity, and technical excellence across each

 - Manage and develop engineering managers, tech leads, and a Principal Engineer, building a high-performing organization that uses AI-assisted development as a core practice

 - Provide hands-on technical leadership by partnering with tech leads on solution design, architectural reviews, production incident management, and complex troubleshooting, while remaining close enough to the systems to guide critical engineering decisions.

 - Partner closely with the VP of Platform Engineering on cross-cutting initiatives including AI coding adoption, release coordination, architectural standards and DevOps.

 - Drive quarterly engineering OKRs focused on AI productivity, code quality, and delivery velocity

 - Participate in senior leadership alongside the COO, VP of Platform Engineering, and product leadership
   

Quality Framework — Entire Engineering Organization

 - Design and implement Bankjoy's engineering quality framework across all eight value streams — setting standards for unit testing, integration testing, automated regression, and release readiness

 - Own a modern multi-platform automation framework covering Angular, Swift, and Kotlin

 - Define and enforce quality gates — explicit go/no-go release criteria that every value stream must meet, including automated coverage thresholds, defect standards, and QE sign-off

 - Lead and develop a QA organization of eight manual QA engineers and two dedicated automation QA engineers, working with the QA manager to elevate the team toward a quality engineering model

 - Establish a test data management layer, environment parity standards, and CI/CD quality integration across all value streams

 - Build a quality dashboard visible to engineering and leadership — automation coverage, open defect counts, regression completion rates — so release readiness is transparent, not judgment-based
   

Release Quality and Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with the Release Manager to implement a structured release gate process — mid-cycle readiness reviews and formal go/no-go decisions with explicit sign-offs from engineering, QA, and product

 - Work closely with Technical Support to close the feedback loop between production issues and engineering priori

source_jobs.title: Client Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
🌐 WHO WE ARE

Bankjoy is a Y Combinator and Bessemer Venture Partner backed FinTech startup. We are changing the way community banks and credit unions do business, enabling them to become tech-driven and digital first. Bankjoy provides modern digital banking products including mobile banking, online banking, online account opening, loan origination, and conversational AI. Our mission is to revolutionize banking tech infrastructure by leading digital transformation at financial institutions — big and small.



Position Overview

We are looking for a Client Success Manager to join our growing CSM team as we scale our client base across credit unions and community banks nationwide.

You will own a portfolio of clients end-to-end — driving product adoption, leading executive business reviews, managing renewals, identifying upsell opportunities, and serving as the primary relationship owner for your accounts. You'll work closely with our Director of Client Success, Implementation, Tech Support, and Product teams to ensure every client gets maximum value from Bankjoy's platform.

The ideal candidate is proactive, relationship-driven, and commercially minded — someone who genuinely cares about client outcomes, knows how to navigate complex organizations, is comfortable engaging executive stakeholders, and can balance a high volume of accounts while taking a strategic approach to long-term client success.

 

Key Responsibilities

Client Relationship Management

 - Own a portfolio of credit union and community bank clients, serving as their primary point of contact at Bankjoy

 - Build and maintain strong, trusted relationships with client stakeholders, from day-to-day administrators to executive sponsors

 - Become a trusted advisor to executive stakeholders by providing strategic recommendations that align Bankjoy solutions with each client's business objectives

 - Conduct regular business reviews and executive meetings to review platform performance, discuss future goals and priorities, identify opportunities for additional value, and align on long-term success

 - Lead onsite client engagements, including preparing executive presentations, facilitating strategic discussions, documenting action items, and ensuring follow-through

 - Serve as the voice of the client internally — ensuring client needs and feedback are surfaced to Product, Engineering, and leadership

Product Adoption & Client Growth

 - Drive product adoption across your portfolio by proactively educating clients on features, best practices, and new releases

 - Develop and execute strategic success plans aligned with each client's business objectives, helping them maximize the value of their Bankjoy investment

 - Identify and lead expansion opportunities within your portfolio by aligning Bankjoy solutions to client business goals and strategic initiatives

 - Track and report on adoption metrics, engagement scores, and account health across your book of business

Renewals & Retention

 - Lead the renewal process for your portfolio, including managing timelines, preparing renewal proposals, presenting pricing recommendations, and negotiating agreements in partnership with the Director of Client Success

 - Proactively identify at-risk accounts and develop mitigation plans before issues escalate

 - Maintain a high client retention rate by ensuring clients consistently see value in Bankjoy's platform

 - Develop and execute account strategies that strengthen executive relationships, improve product adoption, and identify opportunities for long-term growth

Escalation & Issue Resolution

 - Serve as the first line of response for client escalations within your portfolio, triaging issues, coordinating with Tech Support, Product, and Engineering, and providing proactive communication to clients throughout resolution

 - Escalate to the Director of Client Success when situations require senior involvement, providing a clear summary of the issue, actions taken, and recom

notes: FinTech startup with strong engineering focus
